crime.value: Serial poisoning by the Marquise de Brinvilliers, who poisoned her father and others using chemical compounds
crime.quote: the Marquise de Brinvilliers poisoned her father

victim.value: The Marquise's father and other victims, targeted for personal/financial gain within family relationships
victim.quote: the Marquise de Brinvilliers poisoned her father

closed_world.value: 17th century French aristocratic society and the domestic spaces where the poisonings occurred
closed_world.quote: the castle where the Marquise de Brinvilliers poisoned her father

culprit_and_motive.value: The Marquise de Brinvilliers, working with Sainte-Croix, committed poisoning for apparent personal motives
culprit_and_motive.quote: the poisons of Sainte-Croix and Madame de Brinvilliers

resolution.value: The Marquise was executed by burning, with her ashes scattered; justice was achieved through legal proceedings
resolution.quote: taking the body under one arm, all dressed as it was, and the head in his other hand...threw both upon the faggots

suspect_architecture.value: Historical poisoning case with primary suspects: Marquise de Brinvilliers (perpetrator), Chevalier Gaudin de Sainte-Croix (accomplice/supplier), with victims including her father and others through systematic poisoning
suspect_architecture.quote: Are you the Chevalier Gaudin de Sainte-Croix? Captain of the Tracy, regiment? Then I arrest you in the king's name.

red_herring.value: The concealed woman in the carriage creates initial misdirection about who the primary target of arrest is, suggesting she might be the main criminal
red_herring.quote: She was wrapped in cloak and veil, and judging by the precautions she, had taken to hide her face from every eye

clue_fairness.value: Physical evidence of the poison laboratory discovered in 1814 provides concrete proof of the methods used, with detailed description of furnaces, instruments, and chemical substances
clue_fairness.quote: they found a large closet like a laboratory, containing furnaces, chemical instruments, phials hermetically sealed

social_world.value: 17th century French aristocratic society with police authority, religious confessional power, and class privilege allowing crimes to persist within noble circles
social_world.quote: I arrest you in the king's name. What powers have you? This warrant.

reveal_mechanic.value: Chemical analysis potential of the discovered laboratory materials, though the opportunity was lost when the substances were destroyed rather than tested
reveal_mechanic.quote: instead of submitting the ingredients to the tests of modern science, they made away with them all

media_and_audience.value: Public spectacle and religious interpretation of the execution, with people seeking her bones believing she was a saint despite her crimes
media_and_audience.quote: The next day, people were looking for the charred bones of Madame de Brinvilliers, because they said she was a saint.

_meta.title: ebook alexandre dumas pere marquise brinvillier
